prose.overview: Mae Jo Golf Club & Resort is an 18-hole, par-72 course in the Pahpai area of San Sai district, approximately 20 kilometres and 20 minutes north of central Chiang Mai. Opened in 2004 and designed by Thai architect Seni Thirawat, the course is set among the fruit orchards and agricultural land that characterise the San Sai valley — a distinction that gives it a natural, rural atmosphere rare in an era when most new Thai courses favour manicured resort landscaping. The course is surrounded by longan, lychee, pomelo, and tangerine trees whose canopies shade the fairway margins and change character dramatically with the seasons, offering fruit in abundance during the harvest months. The resort component includes 51 rooms in a Lanna-style complex, making it one of the few Chiang Mai-area courses capable of hosting a full golf holiday without requiring outside accommodation. Mae Jo is consistently praised for its subtly challenging greens and narrow, tree-framed fairways.
prose.layout_and_experience:
Seni Thirawat's design favours precision over power. At 6,730 yards from the championship tees, Mae Jo is not among the longest courses in Chiang Mai, but the narrow fairways and strategically placed doglegs ensure that driving accuracy is more valuable than distance. The tree-lined corridors — framed by mature orchard trees rather than the palm or eucalyptus plantings common to newer resort courses — reduce the margin for error and demand disciplined club selection from the tee.

Several holes incorporate tricky doglegs that require the tee shot to be shaped or placed to leave an open angle for the approach. The greens are the course's defining feature: subtly sloping putting surfaces that appear straightforward from a distance but reward careful line-reading. First-time players who underestimate the green contours often leave themselves with difficult downhill putts that can easily run past the hole.

Water hazards appear throughout the round without dominating it — Mae Jo is not a water course in the Gassan Legacy sense, but wet margins add consequence to errant approach shots on key holes. The combination of tight fairways, tricky greens, and seasonal agricultural character makes Mae Jo one of the most authentically Thai golf experiences in the Chiang Mai region.
prose.tips: Ask your caddie to read the greens carefully before putting — the slopes are subtler than they appear from the fairway and local caddie knowledge is particularly valuable here. The green fee of 3,200 THB is the green fee only — add caddie (350 THB) and cart (750 THB) for an all-in total of approximately 4,300 THB. Visit between November and April when the orchard trees are carrying fruit — the seasonal character adds to the experience. The narrow fairways reward a controlled, shaped tee shot over aggressive power; bring a variety of shot shapes. The resort accommodation (51 rooms, Lanna-style) is worth booking if you plan multiple rounds in the Chiang Mai area.
prose.location_and_access: Mae Jo Golf Club & Resort is located in Pahpai, San Sai district, approximately 20 kilometres north of Chiang Mai city centre and around 20 minutes from Chiang Mai International Airport (CNX). Visitors from Bangkok fly to CNX (approximately 1 hour 10 minutes from Suvarnabhumi or Don Mueang) — driving the 700 kilometres from Bangkok is not practical for a golf trip. The course is accessible by Grab taxi from Chiang Mai or by the resort's transfer service; the route heads north from Chiang Mai via Route 11 through San Sai.
prose.rental_cta_context: Heading to Mae Jo Golf Club & Resort for a round among Chiang Mai's fruit orchards? Rent premium clubs in Bangkok — delivered to your hotel before you fly — so you can focus on those subtle greens without the extra weight of airline checked baggage.
